title: Extract AsyncSqliteStore base class from duplicated sqlite lifecycle
analysis:
Three SQLite stores (CostStore, DurableWriteQueue, WriteJournal) implement identical lifecycle patterns: store db_path, init _conn=None, open with mkdir+connect+WAL+optional busy_timeout+DDL schema, close idempotently. CostStore has the most complete lifecycle (context manager, _require_conn guard, double-open guard, no-leak-on-error protection). DurableWriteQueue adds busy_timeout=5000 but uses assert instead of a guard and has no context manager. WriteJournal has a _require_db guard but no busy_timeout, no context manager, and doesn't null _db on close.

The plan extracts a base class into shared/ that captures CostStore's complete lifecycle pattern, plus a standalone apply_wal_pragmas() utility that all three stores can use. CostStore is immediately refactored to subclass it. DurableWriteQueue and WriteJournal (in fused-memory/) are deferred to separate tasks.

The base class uses type(self).__name__ in error messages so CostStore's existing test suite passes without changes to match= patterns. The _schema abstract property returns DDL strings, matching how all three stores define schemas as constants. busy_timeout_ms is an __init__ parameter (default 5000) for explicit per-store configuration.

steps.description: Write failing tests for apply_wal_pragmas: verify it sets journal_mode=WAL and busy_timeout on an aiosqlite connection, and that busy_timeout=0 skips the busy_timeout pragma

Create shared/src/shared/async_sqlite_base.py with apply_wal_pragmas(conn, busy_timeout_ms) async utility function. Sets PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL and PRAGMA busy_timeout={ms}. Skips busy_timeout if ms=0.

Write failing tests for AsyncSqliteBase.__init__ using a concrete test subclass (has _schema property returning a CREATE TABLE statement): verify db_path stored, _conn is None, busy_timeout_ms stored with default 5000
Implement AsyncSqliteBase as ABC with __init__(db_path: Path, *, busy_timeout_ms: int = 5000), abstract _schema property, and stub open/close/context-manager/guard methods

Write failing tests for open(): creates connection, WAL mode is set, busy_timeout matches configured value, schema tables exist, parent dirs created; double-open raises RuntimeError('{ClassName} already opened'); no resource leak if schema executescript fails (conn closed, _conn stays None)

Implement open(): mkdir parents, aiosqlite.connect, call apply_wal_pragmas, executescript(self._schema), set self._conn. Include double-open guard (raise if _conn not None) and try/except to close conn on schema failure
Write failing tests for close(): sets _conn to None after open, idempotent (double-close safe), data persists across close/reopen cycle
Implement close(): if _conn not None, await _conn.close(), set _conn = None
Write failing tests for __aenter__/__aexit__: context manager opens conn, closes on normal exit, closes on exception, returns self from __aenter__
Implement __aenter__ (call open, return self) and __aexit__ (call close)
Write failing tests for _require_conn(): returns connection when open, raises RuntimeError('{ClassName} not opened') when not open, raises after close
Implement _require_conn(): if _conn is None raise RuntimeError(f'{type(self).__name__} not opened'), else return _conn
Add test to test_cost_store.py: CostStore is a subclass of AsyncSqliteBase, CostStore._schema returns the expected DDL string, CostStore.__init__ passes busy_timeout_ms=30000 to base

Refactor CostStore to subclass AsyncSqliteBase: remove duplicated __init__/open/close/__aenter__/__aexit__/_require_conn, add _schema property returning _SCHEMA constant, __init__ calls super().__init__(db_path, busy_timeout_ms=30000). Keep _execute and public API methods unchanged. Run existing test_cost_store.py to verify backward compat.
Update test_public_api.py: add AsyncSqliteBase and apply_wal_pragmas to expected exports in test_init_all_covers_all_module_symbols, add test_async_sqlite_base_all checking module __all__
Update shared/src/shared/__init__.py: import and export AsyncSqliteBase and apply_wal_pragmas from async_sqlite_base module, add to __all__. Add __all__ to async_sqlite_base.py module.

design_decisions.decision: busy_timeout_ms is an __init__ parameter with default 5000, not a class attribute
design_decisions.rationale: More explicit and Python-idiomatic. The value is visible at construction sites (CostStore passes 30000, DurableWriteQueue will pass 5000). Default of 5000 matches DurableWriteQueue's current value and is a reasonable general default.

design_decisions.decision: _schema is an abstract property returning str, not a method
design_decisions.rationale: All three stores define schema as constant strings (_SCHEMA, _CREATE_TABLE+_CREATE_INDEX, SCHEMA_SQL). A property communicates 'this is a fixed value per class' more clearly than a method. Schema SQL is passed to executescript() which handles multi-statement DDL.

design_decisions.decision: Error messages use f'{type(self).__name__} ...' for the class name
design_decisions.rationale: CostStore's existing tests check for 'CostStore already opened' and 'CostStore not opened' via pytest.raises(match=...). Using the concrete class name preserves backward compatibility without requiring test changes.

design_decisions.decision: apply_wal_pragmas is a standalone async function, not a base class method
design_decisions.rationale: DurableWriteQueue and WriteJournal can adopt the utility immediately without refactoring to the full base class. It also follows the task description's explicit request for an extractable utility.

design_decisions.decision: open() uses executescript() for DDL rather than individual execute() calls
design_decisions.rationale: Matches CostStore and WriteJournal's existing pattern. executescript() handles multi-statement DDL (CREATE TABLE + CREATE INDEX) in a single call with implicit commits. DurableWriteQueue's individual execute() pattern can adapt when it adopts the base class.

design_decisions.decision: busy_timeout_ms=0 means skip the PRAGMA entirely, not set timeout to 0
design_decisions.rationale: WriteJournal currently sets no busy_timeout at all. When it eventually adopts the base class, it can pass busy_timeout_ms=0 to preserve its current behavior. This is semantically clearer than using None.

reuse.how: The entire __init__/open/close/__aenter__/__aexit__/_require_conn block becomes the base class implementation almost verbatim, with db_path references generalized and error messages using type(self).__name__

The test structure for init/open/close/context-manager/guards/no-leak-on-error directly informs the base class test structure. A concrete test subclass with a simple schema replaces CostStore in these tests.
Existing conftest already inserts the worktree's src/ at the front of sys.path. New test file test_async_sqlite_base.py inherits this automatically.
aiosqlite>=0.20 already declared as a dependency. No pyproject.toml changes needed.
Existing test_init_all_covers_all_module_symbols unions all module __all__ lists. Adding async_sqlite_base to the union follows the established pattern exactly.
